title: Blu Ray Re Release
wikitext:
[]: Blu-Ray Re-Release             Quote Post by Animejunkie » Thu Nov 18, 2010 3:51 am So just as a question that i know i can get the best answer from here rather than trying to find it on my own... Is there any word on a possible re-release of the entire series on blu-ray... i have recently been equiped with a blu-ray player and now im basically going through all my old animes and being like well its out on blu ray its time to splurge on buying the new better quality blu rays... i know that the rebuilds are on Blu-ray but thats just not the same thing as the original series, also is there any word on a possible EoE Blu-ray release, being the one thing of eva that i actually dont own a copy of to get it in a blu-ray would just make this so much bttr =D... Thanks for any help i get

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Nov 18, 2010 5:31 am Some threads you might want to check out: Evangelion TV series to get Blu-Ray release? and maybe Any Eva releases in widescreen? There's no plans announced to re-release the TV series on BD. There was a re-airing of the TV series a few years ago (I want to say 2007 but not sure) in Japan that was in HD, but that was just upscaled from standard definition.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Nov 20, 2010 1:43 pm That´s why we have Rebuild. Sort of.

[]:             Quote Post by Animejunkie » Sun Nov 21, 2010 4:54 am lol yea i know that thats what rebuild is for... i mainly would like to see EoE on blu-ray. Its the only thing that i dont own yet and would like to get it on Blu-ray over DvD just cause thats the way i am lol.

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Mon Nov 22, 2010 1:47 pm Wasn't there a re-release of the first one or two episodes of the series, re-mastered? I remember reading in New-Type that they re-sourced from frame, but I don't remember at what definition.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Mon Nov 22, 2010 3:50 pm ^ I´d bet all my limbs that little over half of the original Eva cels have already gone missing.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Mon Nov 22, 2010 10:21 pm I WANT IT I WANT IT! I need NGE on blu ray... I... I... I don't even know why *despair*

[]:             Quote Post by Hawq » Sun Nov 28, 2010 8:50 am There is of course the terrible thought that if they were to do it it would cost as much or possibly more than the recent Haruhi blu-ray box

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Mon Nov 29, 2010 6:47 pm when it comes to eva i'm willing to bet that gainax will focus all of their efforts into making rebuil. if they ever want to re code the series for hdtv it'll be after the rebuil project is completed. gainax likes money so if anyone's going to revive an old series (for the 1000th time) it'll be gainax.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Mon Nov 29, 2010 8:32 pm berto wrote: when it comes to eva i'm willing to bet that gainax will focus all of their efforts into making rebuil. if they ever want to re code the series for hdtv it'll be after the rebuil project is completed. gainax likes money so if anyone's going to revive an old series (for the 1000th time) it'll be gainax.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Nov 29, 2010 8:51 pm Clover wrote: >Implying that rebuild is made by gainax lolno

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Thu Dec 02, 2010 12:28 am Actually, I recall reading Anno getting fucking livid at Gainax for making a Gunbuster pachinko machine without his permission. Or putting his name on it, or something. And that the people at Gainax that managed licensing for the Evangelion property had up and left. Maybe Gainax can still create a new Evangelion product using their own property -- HD sourcing from original cells or whatever -- or maybe all chance of an HD series is gone for good. Thinking back on all the animator tricks they used throughout the series, I'm not entirely sure HD would really enhance the experience. I seem to recall them overlaying a TWAIN scanner instruction manual's plaintext on a body scan of Rei Ayanami, for example...

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Thu Dec 02, 2010 1:21 am Rights issues aside, I wonder if the Renewal remastering work was done at a resolution higher than DVD. High-definition TV isn't as new in Japan as it is in the US.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Dec 02, 2010 9:24 am drinian wrote: Rights issues aside, I wonder if the Renewal remastering work was done at a resolution higher than DVD. High-definition TV isn't as new in Japan as it is in the US.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Sat Dec 04, 2010 10:42 pm I would quite like to see them do a trace job, re-draw and colour every cel in HD dubbed with the original audio. Exactly the same show scene for scene but brighter, crisper and clearer... With maybe a few goofs fixed.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Dec 05, 2010 5:04 pm skikes wrote: I would quite like to see them do a trace job, re-draw and colour every cel in HD dubbed with the original audio. Exactly the same show scene for scene but brighter, crisper and clearer... With maybe a few goofs fixed.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Dec 05, 2010 6:53 pm skikes wrote: I would quite like to see them do a trace job, re-draw and colour every cel in HD dubbed with the original audio. Exactly the same show scene for scene but brighter, crisper and clearer... With maybe a few goofs fixed.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Dec 05, 2010 7:10 pm Don't expect a Blu-Ray release of Evangelion anytime soon. At least not until DVD is phased out. The main reason is that Evangelion was shot on 16mm film. The difference between that and 35mm, which translates well to 1080p, is that you're only getting one quarter the resolution. 16mm is better suited to 480p. So while they could upscale it, you won't be seeing a significant increase in quality like we did from VHS to DVD, or from the Perfect to Platinum releases. There's no real reason to put it on Blu-Ray now. Blu-Ray is expensive, and doesn't have the proper saturation, and the masters can't make proper use of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sun Dec 05, 2010 7:22 pm A Blu-ray release is just a matter of time.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Dec 05, 2010 8:55 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Blu-Ray is expensive, and doesn't have the proper saturation

Is there any word on a possible re-release of the entire series on blu-ray... i have recently been equiped with a blu-ray player and now im basically going through all my old animes and being like well its out on blu ray its time to splurge on
